_comment: Next Best Action rules for Module 2 (ARCHITECTURE.md §6.5). Maps detected issue_type -> recommendation, execution-mode policy, and optimization factors (§8.11). Merge behaviour (§6.6) is keyed by root_cause_group: rules sharing a group merge into one primary action; different groups stay separate. The AI/Mock-Data subteam owns the optimization_factors + forecast; the safety/execution-mode decision is finalised by the SE backend's safety engine (§13).

rules.recommended_action: Restrict public access, patch or isolate the workload, and notify the security team.
rules.rollback_note: Re-open access only after the vulnerability is confirmed patched. Isolation can be reverted by restoring the previous security group.

rules.recommended_action: Restrict public storage access, review the bucket/container access policy, and notify the owner/security team.
rules.rollback_note: Access policy change is reversible by restoring the previous bucket policy if the exposure was intended.

rules.recommended_action: Limit usage during inactive hours: schedule shutdown outside working hours and resize the workload.
rules.rollback_note: The workload can be restarted manually or automatically if needed. Resource limits can be restored to the previous configuration.

rules.recommended_action: Reschedule the job to a lower-carbon window, reduce frequency, or resize compute.
rules.rollback_note: The original schedule can be restored at any time; rescheduling does not alter job output.

rules.recommended_action: Investigate logs, notify DevOps/SRE, and create an incident if in production.
rules.rollback_note: Notification/ticket creation is non-destructive and requires no rollback.

rules.recommended_action: Enable monitoring, create an owner ticket, and add the workload to the dashboard.
rules.rollback_note: Monitoring can be disabled again if enabled in error; no workload disruption.

rules.recommended_action: Review usage, notify the owner, and resize or schedule shutdown.
rules.rollback_note: Resource limits can be restored to the previous configuration if utilisation rises.

merge_policy._comment: §6.6: rules in the same root_cause_group merge into one primary recommendation. Primary = most specific operational trigger; ties broken by severity then by least-permissive execution mode. Different groups never merge.

optimization_factors_reference._comment: Canonical table from §8.11. Engine picks the factor set tied to the chosen recommendation_type; ranges are collapsed to the conservative single value baked into each rule above.
